prompt_md:
Your document-extraction feature was shipped a year ago by **calling a hosted LLM API** behind a thin abstraction layer. Since then: volume grew from 2,000 to 900,000 invoices/month so the **per-call price is now your largest cost line**; finance auto-posts low-risk invoices so the **tolerable error rate dropped**; and you have accumulated **a year of labeled extractions**. Analysis shows most invoices follow a **handful of known templates**, with only a minority in genuinely novel layouts.

You can make **one** sourcing change next. Which change best fits the L2 lens — and why?

decision_choices: Keep everything on the general hosted API unchanged, since it already works
Route the known-template invoices to a deterministic parser, and fine-tune a smaller model for the novel-layout remainder, keeping the existing abstraction layer
Build a fully custom in-house model for every invoice, since owning the model is always cheapest
Switch to the most proprietary specialist SaaS suite with no abstraction layer to simplify operations
reason_choices: Because shrinking the AI surface to the irreducibly fuzzy step lowers cost and raises testability, fine-tuning leverages the now-available labeled data for higher accuracy, and the abstraction keeps switching cost low and the decision reversible
Because keeping the unchanged API is best, as per-call price is irrelevant at any volume
Because building in-house always has the lowest total cost of ownership regardless of volume or differentiation
Because a proprietary suite with no abstraction maximizes lock-in, which lowers switching costs
